Valley Fever cases are at an all time high
People and animals can get sick when they breathe in dust that contains the Valley fever fungus. This fungus usually infects the lungs and can cause respiratory symptoms including cough, fever, chest pain, and tiredness.
Learn More
Waste Hunger Not Food
Kern County Public Health oversees and coordinates the transport of donated food from schools to distribution sites where the food will be made available to residents in need.
Learn More
Public Health Catalog
Our catalog showcases Kern County Public Health and our diverse services. We prioritize community care through home visits, a mobile clinic, and safety inspections. Programs like Know Your Numbers and Waste Hunger Not Food provide health education and nutritious food. We're here to serve and enhance your quality of life, working together for a healthier Kern County!
Learn More
Brush, Book, Bed.
Recommended by the American Academy of Pediatrics, this simple nighttime routine helps children wind down and build healthy habits.

Thrive Like a Poppy
Health is a journey and Thrive Like a Poppy celebrates every step of it. This year-long initiative uses the life cycle of a poppy to spotlight essential public health services and seasonal wellness tips for every age, reminding our community that it’s possible to thrive at every stage of life.

Kern County Public Health Conducts Unpermitted Food Vendor Education and Enforcement Operation
Kern County Public Health, in partnership with the Kern County Sheriff's Office and Bakersfield Police Department, conducted a coordinated education and enforcement operation on February 28, 2026 targeting food vendors operating without the proper health permits within Kern County.
